name: Nur Islamic School of Louisville
mission:
Our Mission
Our holistic education aims to provide the environment for the total and balanced development of every student in every sphere of learning –...
theoryOfChange: Holistic education providing total and balanced development (spiritual, moral, imaginative, intellectual, cultural, aesthetic, emotional, physical) directed toward attaining a conscious relationship with God; aims to cultivate lifelong learners with strong and balanced character connected with their Creator and serving humanity.
scoreSummary: NUR ISLAMIC SCHOOL OF LOUISVILLE shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions a lack of external data regarding cost-per-beneficiary or standardized academic outcomes, but the score details show that the 'Evidence & Outcomes' and 'Theory of Change' components are maxed out (6/6 and 7/7 respectively). This suggests a disconnect between the rationale's emphasis and the actual scoring breakdown.

The rationale does not explicitly mention the risk deduction due to the small board size (2 members), which is a key factor affecting the overall score. While the overall score rationale mentions 'minor risk deductions applied,' it doesn't specify the reason.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

score_details.score_summary: NUR ISLAMIC SCHOOL OF LOUISVILLE shows good impact and moderate alignment (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The official donation pages and other relevant sections of the Nur Islamic School of Louisville website do not explicitly mention 'zakat' as a type of accepted donation or have a dedicated zakat fund. While they offer a 'Financial Aid Fund' to support students, it is not explicitly stated to be for zakat-eligible recipients. (Source: https://www.nurislamicschool.org/donate.html)

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated as medium-low (24/50) because the school serves a specific local community in Louisville rather than addressing global educational crises [3][6]. While it provides a vital service for Muslim families seeking sadaqah-eligible giving opportunities, its specialized scope may not fit donors looking for broad-scale systemic change [8].
dimension_explanations.impact: The school demonstrates moderate impact through its capacity-building model, maintaining a 100.0% program expense ratio and 12.2 months of working capital to support its K-12 operations [1][2]. While the financial health is robust, there is a lack of external data regarding cost-per-beneficiary or standardized academic outcomes [1].

baseline_narrative.summary: Located in Louisville, Kentucky, the Nur Islamic School offers a comprehensive educational program including an Early Childhood Center, Elementary School, and K-12 private schooling [1][3]. The institution focuses on the balanced development of students across all spheres of learning to foster a complete educational experience [3][4].

alignment.improvement: The school could improve alignment for specific donors by <cite id="15">clearly stating its sadaqah policies</cite> and any financial aid pathways for needy students.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the school's longevity, as it has been <cite id="1">operating since 1999</cite> and maintains <cite id="8">dual academic accreditation</cite>. However, credibility is slightly hampered by a <cite id="12">board consisting of only two members</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the school should <cite id="12">expand its board of directors</cite> to include more independent members and publish annual impact reports.

impact.explanation: The school demonstrates high efficiency with a <cite id="5">100.0% program ratio</cite> and <cite id="10">12.2 months of working capital</cite>. It successfully integrates <cite id="7">Islamic studies into a K-12 framework</cite>.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="13">publishing student graduation rates</cite> and college placement data to quantify long-term success.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value the preservation of Islamic identity through formal K-12 education and prefer supporting institutions with zero fundraising overhead.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: While the school is accredited and has a clear mission, it does not publicly track specific long-term outcome metrics.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The school uses a holistic model to develop students intellectually and spiritually through a combined curriculum.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Outcome data would help donors understand the long-term return on their investment in student success.

strengths.detail: The school directs <cite id="5">1.0% of its expenses to programs</cite>, supported by <cite id="9">$0.00 spent on fundraising</cite> for every dollar raised.
NISL maintains <cite id="10">12.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a significant buffer for long-term operational sustainability.
The institution holds <cite id="8">accreditation from Cognia and CISNA</cite>, ensuring rigorous external validation of its educational model.

strengths_deep_dive: Nur Islamic School of Louisville demonstrates a rare level of fiscal discipline for a private educational institution. By maintaining a <cite id="5">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>, the school ensures that tuition and donations are utilized entirely for student development and school operations. This efficiency is further highlighted by a <cite id="9">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, suggesting that the school relies on organic community support and program service revenue rather than expensive marketing campaigns. Furthermore, the school's <cite id="11">100% independent board</cite> provides a layer of objective oversight that is critical for maintaining public trust.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1999</cite>, Nur Islamic School of Louisville (NISL) serves as a cornerstone for Islamic education in Kentucky. The institution provides a <cite id="2">holistic educational environment</cite> for students from early childhood through 12th grade. The school integrates <cite id="3">divine revelation with rational knowledge</cite>, aiming for the balanced development of every student. Financially, the organization operates with remarkable efficiency. It reported <cite id="4">$2,796,768 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Most notably, the school maintains a <cite id="5">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>. This means every dollar spent is directed toward educational services rather than administrative overhead. The school has shown significant financial growth, achieving a <cite id="6">3-year revenue CAGR of 29.0%</cite>. This growth reflects increasing community trust and a widening reach within the Louisville area. Beyond standard academics, the curriculum includes <cite id="7">Arabic, Quran, and Islamic Studies</cite>, ensuring students maintain a strong religious identity alongside competitive schooling. The school is also <cite id="8">accredited by Cognia and CISNA</cite>, which validates its commitment to high academic standards and continuous improvement.
